Left drag: create note
Alt + left drag: create Bezier/Glide note (shape is selected by Curve)
Left click: move playhead / select note
Ctrl + left click: add/remove multiple selection
Shift + left click: range selection
Right click: delete note
Space: play/pause
Snap: snap to BPM grid

help.header: Quick help for the current editor. Press F1 anytime to open this window.
help.quick_start.title: Quick Start
help.quick_start.body:
Quick Start

1. Open an audio file:
   File -> Open Audio

2. Wait for the CQT spectrogram to load.
   If the audio is long, analysis may take a while. Cached files load faster.

3. Create notes:
   - Left drag on the spectrogram to create a normal note.
   - Alt + left drag to create a Curve/Glide note.

4. Check playback:
   - Space: play/pause
   - Ctrl + Space: stop
   - Song Vol: original audio volume
   - Note Vol: note preview volume

5. Export:
   - File -> Export MIDI
   - File -> Export ADOFAI Hz

Basic idea:
AdopyHzEditor is not a fully automatic transcription tool. It is a manual editor that helps you read pitches from a spectrogram and convert them into MIDI or ADOFAI Hz Charts.

Tip:
If the screen is noisy, try changing Analysis profile, Harmonics, Contrast, Gamma, or Display mode.

Pitch controls:
- Preview Oct changes only the preview sound.
- Export Oct / Export Semi change only MIDI and ADOFAI export.

Blank workspace:
You can also place notes before opening audio. The initial screen is a black placeholder workspace.
help.controls.title: Controls / Shortcuts
help.controls.body:
Controls / Shortcuts

Mouse:
- Left drag:
  Create a note.

- Alt + left drag:
  Create a Curve/Glide note.

- Left click note:
  Select note.

- Left click empty area:
  Move playhead.

- Ctrl + left click:
  Add/remove note from selection.

- Shift + left click:
  Range selection.

- Right click:
  Delete nearest note.

Editing:
- Delete:
  Delete selected notes.

- Ctrl + A:
  Select all notes.

- Esc:
  Clear selection.

- Ctrl + C / X / V:
  Copy / cut / paste.

- Ctrl + Z:
  Undo.

- Ctrl + Y or Ctrl + Shift + Z:
  Redo.

Playback:
- Space:
  Play / pause.

- Ctrl + Space:
  Stop.

- Left / Right:
  Seek by 1 second.

- Shift + Left / Right:
  Seek by 5 seconds.

View:
- 1:
  Spectrogram focus.

- 2:
  Note focus.

- 3:
  Both.

- Tab:
  Cycle display mode.

Pitch navigation:
- Up / Down:
  Move pitch view by 1 semitone.

- W / S or Ctrl + Up / Down:
  Move pitch view by 1 octave.

Selected note movement:
- Ctrl + Shift + Left / Right:
  Move selected notes horizontally.

- Ctrl + Shift + Up / Down:
  Move selected notes by semitone.

Target Angle:
- Ctrl + Alt + A:
  Apply Target Angle to selected notes.
help.adofai_export.title: ADOFAI Export Guide
help.adofai_export.body:
ADOFAI Export Guide

AdopyHzEditor has three ADOFAI Hz export methods.

1. Angle Compression
This is the main Hz Chart method.
It calculates Keycount from Hz, BPM, and duration, then compresses the chart using a calculated angle.

Useful when:
- You want compact zips.
- You want to control the visual angle.
- You want to use Target Angle override.

Important options:
- Base BPM:
  BPM used for the Keycount calculation.

- Change x mode:
  Controls how the change_x value is chosen.

- Target Angle:
  Per-note/zip visual angle override.
  Angle-only ignores this because Angle-only uses angle itself as the pitch.

- Final tile mode:
  Controls the final fractional tile.

2. Direct 180°
This uses:

  BPM = Hz * 60

It usually produces visually simple 180° tiles.
It is easy to understand, but it can create many BPM changes.

Useful when:
- You want predictable straight tiles.
- You do not need angle compression.

3. Angle-only
This uses one global BPM and represents pitch only with tile angles.

Higher pitch:
  shorter time between hits
  smaller angle

Lower pitch:
  longer time between hits
  larger angle

Useful when:
- You want fewer SetSpeed events.
- You want a chart where pitch is represented mainly by angles.

Phase-continuous glide:
For Curve/Glide notes, this avoids splitting the glide into many short fixed-Hz notes.
Instead, it treats the glide as one continuous frequency curve and places tiles by accumulated phase.

Simple explanation:
Old method:
  smooth curve -> many short fixed-Hz notes -> choppy output

New method:
  smooth curve -> integrate frequency -> place tiles per sound cycle -> smoother output

Debug Preview:
Use Debug Preview before exporting if you want to inspect:
- keycount
- angle
- target_angle
- phase_continuous
- final_bpm
- warnings

Export pitch controls:
- Preview Oct is ignored by export.
- Export Oct and Export Semi are applied before calculating Hz/ADOFAI tiles.
- Use these when the chart should be exported in a different octave without moving notes on screen.

Project audio / songOffset workflow:
- When exporting ADOFAI, Use project audio as ADOFAI song writes the current audio file name into settings.songFilename.
- Copy song next to .adofai copies the audio file into the export folder.
- Use first note start sets settings.songOffset to the first note's start time.
- This is useful because Hz export normalizes the generated chart to start at the first note, while the audio file may have silence or intro before that point.

Portable project audio loading:
Projects now save both the original audio path and a project-relative audio path.
When a project is moved with its audio file, AdopyHzEditor tries the relative path first.
If the audio is still missing, it asks whether to locate the audio manually or load notes only.

Harmony / Polyrhythm:
This mode creates a root pitch impulse train and an extra harmony impulse train, then merges them into one serial ADOFAI tile path.
It is mainly a visual Harmony Charting mode, useful for chord-like or polyrhythmic-looking structures.

Options:
- Harmony: octave, fifth, major third, minor third, lower octave, or custom interval
- Harmony custom: semitone interval used when Harmony is custom
- Harmony epsilon: tiny time separation for events that would otherwise be simultaneous

The preview/export is still one ADOFAI path, not multiple lanes.
help.curve_glide.title: Curve / Glide Guide
help.curve_glide.body:
Curve / Glide Guide

Curve/Glide notes are for continuously changing pitch.

Create:
- Hold Alt and drag on the spectrogram.

Interpolation modes:
- bezier_pitch:
  Bezier curve in MIDI/semitone space.
  Good for musical pitch movement.

- linear_pitch:
  Straight line in MIDI/semitone space.
  The pitch moves evenly by semitone.

- linear_hz:
  Straight line in Hz space.
  Physical frequency changes at a constant speed.

- bezier_hz:
  Bezier curve in Hz space.
  Useful for effect-like glides.

Apply interpolation to existing notes:
1. Select Curve/Glide notes.
2. Choose Interp.
3. Press Apply Interp.

Why phase-continuous export matters:
Even if a curve looks smooth, exporting it as many short fixed-Hz notes can sound stepped/choppy.
Phase-continuous export avoids that by using the continuous frequency curve directly.

Recommended default:
- For normal musical glides:
  linear_pitch or bezier_pitch

- For effect-like frequency sweeps:
  linear_hz or bezier_hz

- For smoother ADOFAI export:
  enable Phase-continuous glide
help.troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
help.troubleshooting.body:
Troubleshooting

The app freezes when loading audio:
- CQT analysis runs in the background, but very large files can still take time.
- Try Fast or Normal analysis profile.
- If you only need the notes, load the project as notes-only.

The spectrogram is black:
- If you loaded a project without audio, this is intentional.
- A black placeholder is shown so old audio/spectrogram data is not reused accidentally.

The audio does not play:
- The playback audio may still be loading.
- Wait a moment and press Space again.
- Check Song Vol.
- If running from source, confirm sounddevice is installed.

The pitch line is hard to see:
- Try Harmonics: soft or strong.
- Adjust Contrast and Gamma.
- Try a different Display mode or Colormap.

Exported ADOFAI is huge:
- High notes create many tiles.
- Long Curve/Glide notes with phase-continuous Direct 180° or Angle Compression can create many SetSpeed events.
- Try Angle-only mode if file size matters.

Updater / version check:
- This build does not auto-download updates.
- Use Options -> Open Releases to open the GitHub Releases page manually.

PyInstaller build issue:
- Include locales with:
  --add-data "locales;locales"

- Do not use --collect-all PySide6 unless needed.
  This app uses QtWidgets, not QML/QtQuick.
help.about.title: About
help.about.body:
AdopyHzEditor

Version:
{version}

Purpose:
AdopyHzEditor is a spectrogram-based manual note tracing editor for MIDI and ADOFAI Hz Chart export.

Repository / Releases:
{releases_url}

Main workflow:
Audio file
-> CQT spectrogram
-> manual note tracing / curve editing
-> preview playback
-> MIDI export / ADOFAI Hz Chart export

Notes:
- This is not a fully automatic transcription tool.
- Some technical labels are intentionally kept in English.
- Debug Preview values are mostly for advanced users.
export.debug_preview.tooltip: Preview Hz/BPM/angle/Keycount/fractional tile values before export
help.pitch_export.title: Pitch / Octave Controls
help.pitch_export.body:
Pitch / Octave Controls

AdopyHzEditor now separates preview pitch from export pitch.

Preview Oct:
- Affects note preview sound only.
- Does not move notes on screen.
- Does not affect MIDI export.
- Does not affect ADOFAI export.

Export Oct:
- Affects MIDI and ADOFAI export only.
- Does not affect note preview sound.
- Does not move notes on screen.

Export Semi:
- Affects MIDI and ADOFAI export only.
- Shifts exported pitch by semitone.

The final export pitch shift is:

  export_pitch = note_pitch + Export Oct * 12 + Export Semi

Examples:
- Export Oct +1, Export Semi +0:
  export one octave higher

- Export Oct +0, Export Semi -1:
  export one semitone lower

- Preview Oct +1, Export Oct +0:
  preview sounds one octave higher, but exported file stays unchanged

This separation is useful when:
- the preview sound is too low/high while editing
- the ADOFAI Hz export needs a different octave
- you want to test charting at another pitch without moving notes on screen

Old projects:
Older projects used a single note_octave setting. If no new export pitch settings exist, it is loaded into both Preview Oct and Export Oct to preserve old behavior.
